scenario: I've had a blinding headache for a few days
patient_utterances: [Clinician asked: "There's no <INAUDIBLE_SPEECH/>. , hello?"] . Hi there.

[Clinician asked: "Hello there. Um, thanks for, thanks for, being here. Um, before I go any further, can I uh, confirm your name and your age please?"] Yes, it's um Adriana Katherine . And I'm twenty six year old.
[Clinician asked: "<INAUDIBLE_SPEECH/> OK, Adriana, and how can I help you this afternoon?"] Well, I have this like really crazy headache that's been going on for a few days. And it really annoys me.
[Clinician asked: "Ohh dear, OK. , when, when did it exactly start, this headache?"] Eh, around three days ago, maybe.
[Clinician asked: "Three days ago, OK. And whereabout in your head, is this pain?"] Um, it's basically like, it kind of feels all over my head, but like mainly, um, around my right eye. Um

[Clinician asked: "<INAUDIBLE_SPEECH/>, OK. Um, and is the, headache there all the time, or does it come and go?"] Um it's, it kind of comes and goes. Um but, I mean it is there all the time, but  like sometimes it's stronger.

[Clinician asked: "OK, so it comes and, fine, OK. And if I was to ask you on a scale of one to ten, um, ten being the worst type of pain you ever had. How would you rate this headache?"] Ohh it's probably a eight.

[Clinician asked: "Eight. And if I was to ask you describe the headache to me, um, in terms of the nature of the pain. Is it sharp? Is it dull? Is it crampy? Is it throbbing?"] Um, it's kind of, it's dull but sometimes it gets a bit sharper. So basically it like, it developed gradually, I guess. It's like the first day it just started when I was at work.

[Clinician asked: "Mm-hmm."] And by the end of the day it was like pretty, pretty, like it was a lot, basically. And then I was thinking OK, maybe I'll just go to sleep. And when I woke up the next morning like, it was even, even worse basically. So
[Clinician asked: "Mm-hmm. So, you think the headache is getting worse over the last three days?"] Yeah.
[Clinician asked: "OK. Have you tried anything which has made the headache better?"] Um so I tried uh Ibuprofen. Um so I took, like a few. Um for the last two days, basically.
[Clinician asked: "Did that help?"] But it doesn't really help, no. In in the past it did help me, which is why I decided to try it. But
[Clinician asked: "OK, all right. And except for the headaches, any other symptoms at all?"] Um

[Clinician asked: "So, example any, problems your eyesight or your vision?"] Not really, but just when it started, I I got this like weird shooting lights. And like now like light is quite annoying, my eyes.
[Clinician asked: "So when you say annoying, does it hurt to look, uh, look at bright lights?"] Yeah, yeah. It hurts. So
[Clinician asked: ", OK. And um, have you had any loss of vision or blurry vision?"] Not loss of vision, I wouldn't say. It's just like some lights from, shooting lights <INAUDIBLE_SPEECH/>.
[Clinician asked: "Mmm, , OK. Any nausea or any vomiting?"] Um, well I'm like basically, I I'm sick with the headache. But I, no vomiting.
[Clinician asked: "When you say sick, you mean you feel sick? OK. Yeah, no vomiting, OK. Any neck pain or neck stiffness?"] Yeah. No.
[Clinician asked: "OK. And any difficulty with speaking or, um, speech?"] No.
[Clinician asked: "Any difficulty , with weakness in your arms and legs?"] No, it's hard to focus though. So, I I had to take a couple of days off work, actually.
[Clinician asked: "Ohh really? OK. OK, yeah probably, well I'll, I'll ask you a bit more about your work later on. Um, any recent head injuries at all?"] Uh, no.
[Clinician asked: "OK. And have you had anything like this before in the past?"] Yeah I mean, headaches, you mean.

[Clinician asked: "Or, uh, or similar, similar to, similar to this kind of headache, <INAUDIBLE_SPEECH/>?"] Yeah. I mean I had. But, usually after I I take uh Ibuprofen it kind of goes away. So, but this time it's just not. <INAUDIBLE_SPEECH/>.
[Clinician asked: "<INAUDIBLE_SPEECH/> not, OK. All right. Um, any temperatures, or fevers?"] No.
[Clinician asked: "Any rashes at all, on your skin?"] Mm, no not that I've noticed.
[Clinician asked: "OK. And just moving forwards. Are you otherwise fit and well, Adriana?"] Am I what, sorry?
[Clinician asked: "You fit and well? , do you have any other medical history?"] Yeah, I think so. It's just the headache.
[Clinician asked: "Any history of migraines at all?"] Not myself, I've never had a migraine. But my sister and my mom have them quite frequently.
[Clinician asked: "OK. Um, fine. I'll ask you a bit more about that in just a second. Before I go any further, can I ask, do you take any regular medication?"] Uh, no no.
[Clinician asked: "Are you on the pill? OK. Do you have any allergies to any medications?"] Mm not that I know of.
[Clinician asked: "OK. So you mentioned your mother and sister both have migraines."] Yeah.

[Clinician asked: "Are they, is that normally well-controlled or are they um, having difficulty, controlling their symptoms?"] Um so my sister's, like better. Like it's kind of, it's very rare. But my mom's is like quite bad, um usually when there's like a season changing and stuff like that. It's, yeah.
[Clinician asked: "Mm-hmm, OK."] Takes her out for a few days, basically.

[Clinician asked: "Ohh right, OK, fine. Um, socially, tell me a bit more about your life, outside of, um, outside of, work. So , who lives at home with you?"] Uh I live with housemates. So, three of them. So we're four .

[Clinician asked: "OK. Excellent. And are you working at the moment? You mentioned this , impacting on your work."] Yeah so I work, well I work in IT. So I work with computers a lot. So it kind of is difficult to focus. Um I had to like reduce the uh, brightness of my screen.
[Clinician asked: "Mmm. OK. Do you wear glasses?"] Yes, I do.
[Clinician asked: "Have you had any, uh, have you had any recent eye check, done? Eye test?"] No, uh I've been wearing them since I was five , five year old.  So, it's quite a long time.

[Clinician asked: "OK, all right. Something for you to bear in mind if you feel that you are, your eyes are straining. Um I would always recommend having a repeat eye test done. Um, have you been feeling more stressed or anxious at work recently?"] <INAUDIBLE_SPEECH/> Well, it's been a bit stressful, kicking off a new project. But um, yeah nothing out of the ordinary, really.
[Clinician asked: "OK, all right. Great. Um, regarding smoking or alcohol. Do you drink or smoke at all?"] I socially smoke. And I drink  only socially.

[Clinician asked: "But only socially? OK. Right. Now um, Adriana, do you have any ideas as to what could be causing your symptoms? Have you, have you read anything online, or spoken to people?"] Um Not really. Um <INAUDIBLE_SPEECH/> at first I thought it's stress, or maybe I didn't drink enough water. Because sometimes when I don't drink enough water, I I like get headaches. Um
[Clinician asked: "Mm-hmm."] But, I don't think that's it. And I'm like worried, it's, I don't want it to be a migraine. Because I know what it's like, from my mom and my sister.

[Clinician asked: "<INAUDIBLE_SPEECH/> Mmm."] And it's really annoying. So I I really don't want to have that. And I know it's genetic. So I've researched that. Um but I don't know if that is the case. So, yeah.

[Clinician asked: "OK. Is it anything in particular you were hoping I could do for you today?"] Yeah maybe just kinda, if you have any recommendations of  that I can take something stronger. Because apparently, Ibuprofen doesn't help me. Uh
[Clinician asked: "OK."] Usually it gets resolved if I have headache in like, after like one or two days. But currently it, it didn't help me.
[Clinician asked: "How many tablets did you take of Ibuprofen?"] Uh, three a day.

[Clinician asked: "Three a day, OK. All right. Um, so uh, Adriana, so um, really what I think is going on here. I , uh, unfortunately I do think this could be, a headache very similar to a migraine."] <INAUDIBLE_SPEECH/>

[Clinician asked: "Um, the reason I say that is because the history is very suggestive of it, given your mum and your sister also migraines. I think it's something for us to think about. Um, now I'm not saying it's gonna, keep happening, but, it may just be a one-off. But it's certainly worth treating it today stronge"] Ohh right.

[Clinician asked: "So, so , there's definitely room for, improvement there. Or, I can prescribe you something stronger, like Naproxen, which is another anti-inflammatory, which can be helpful."] Mm-hmm.

[Clinician asked: "Um, we can also add in other, painkillers like, Paracetamol, or Codeine. Which might be helpful as well."] Mm, yeah. I think  is what my mom takes for when she has um, um migraines.

[Clinician asked: "OK. And I think it's , certainly worth looking into that, so I can give you a , prescription for that today. Um, with regards to um, the next few days. It may be worth keeping a headache diary so we can try and work out what's, you know, if there's any triggers which could be causing your headaches "] Mm-hmm.

[Clinician asked: "And, and, you know I'd like you to come back and see me, in about uh, a week's time, week to two weeks' time. If you are having , more frequent episodes of these type of headaches. We can think about giving you medications as, prophylactic. Which basically means, medications to stop you from having "] Mm, OK.

[Clinician asked: "OK. In the meantime if your headaches aren't getting any better, or they're getting worse. Um, you know, I want you to give me a call, or come back and see me sooner. OK?"] OK. Yeah.
[Clinician asked: "So, if you're having any problems with your vision, or weakness in your arms and legs, or you're just not feeling very well. Just give me a call, and come back and see me."] OK.
[Clinician asked: "All right?"] Great.
[Clinician asked: "Great. Well, have a good day."] Thank you. You too. Bye!
gold_note:
3/7 hx developed headache - all over of my head and mainly over right eye. Constant, severity 8/10, dull ache with associated sharp pain, gradual onset. Progressively worsening. Has tried ibuprofen with limited relief. 
No visual sx
Visual aura prior to headaches. Some photophobia. 
Feels nauseous, no vomiting
No neck pain/stiffness
No speech disturbances 
No arm or leg weakness
No head injury 
No fevers
No rashes 

PMH: Nil 
DH: Nil
NKDA
FH: mother and sister - migraines
SH: lives with housemates, works in IT, wears glasses, denies stress. Socially smoke/EtOH 

ICE: ?related to stress/dehydration. Concerned about migraines. 

Imp: migraines 

Plan:
1.	Paracetamol/NSAIDs regular 
2.	Headache diary
3.	Review in 1/52 - discuss prophylactic Rx if necessary
4.	Patient aware of red flag sx - knows to seek urgent medical attention if present

gold_sections.PRESENTING_COMPLAINT: I've had a blinding headache for a few days
gold_sections.HIGHLIGHTS:
headache
Visual aura
photophobia
nauseous
mother and sister - migraines
migraines

naive.response_text:
Adriana, I truly understand how frustrating and concerning this headache must be for you. It’s important to acknowledge how strong and brave you are for taking the time to share your symptoms with me. Based on what you’ve described, it does sound like this could be a migraine, especially considering your family history. Migraines can be quite challenging, but the good news is that we have some effective treatments to help manage the pain and prevent future episodes.

I recommend trying stronger pain relievers like Naproxen or a combination of Paracetamol and Codeine, which can be very helpful for migraines. It’s also a good idea to keep track of your symptoms and any potential triggers in a headache diary. This can provide valuable insights into what might be causing your headaches and how to avoid them in the future.

Please make sure to follow up with me in about a week to two weeks, or sooner if your symptoms worsen or if you experience any new issues like vision changes or severe weakness. In the meantime, if anything feels off or if the pain doesn’t improve, don’t hesitate to reach out. You’re not alone in this, and we’re here to support you through it. Take care, and I hope you feel better soon.

strong_prompt.response_text:
Thank you for sharing your concerns with me, Adriana. It sounds like you're in a lot of discomfort, and I can understand how worrying this must be, especially given your family history of migraines. Having a severe headache for three days is exhausting, and I want to work with you to find some relief.

From what you've described, the pattern of your symptoms—aching around your right eye, sensitivity to light, and the fact that it's not improving with ibuprofen—does make me think of migraines. Since your mom and sister have them, it’s something we should consider. Migraines can run in families, and they often present with these kinds of symptoms [PMID ?]. 

For now, I’ll prescribe you a stronger anti-inflammatory, like naproxen, which can sometimes help with migraines better than ibuprofen [PMID ?]. We might also add some codeine if needed. In the meantime, I’d like you to keep track of what’s happening with your headaches—when they occur, what makes them worse or better, and any other symptoms you notice. This will help us figure out if there are triggers we can avoid.

Let’s plan to check in in about a week to see how you’re doing. If the headaches aren’t getting better or if they get worse, or if you have any new symptoms like vision changes or weakness, please give me a call right away. I want to make sure we’re on the right track.

Before you go, can you tell me in your own words what we’ve agreed on today? That way I can make sure everything makes sense to you.
